source: # Manual-import seed for Nigeria fertilizer consumption (state × year × nutrient).
# WHY THIS FILE EXISTS:
#   Nigeria publishes fertilizer-consumption statistics via NBS publications and
#   the AfricaFertilizer/IFDC Fertilizer Statistics Overview series
#   exposes machine-readable APIs. See ../canonical/AFRICA_DATA_SOURCES.md for
#   the full landscape audit.
# HOW TO POPULATE:
#   1. Locate the latest Nigeria fertilizer-consumption table. Candidates:
#      - NBS publication "Nigerian Fertilizer Consumption" (state-level
#        per agent's catalog research; direct URL not yet located on
#        nigerianstat.gov.ng — check eLibrary).
#      - IFDC "Fertilizer Statistics Overview: Nigeria 2018-2022" PDF
#        (api.hub.ifdc.org/server/api/core/bitstreams/<uuid>).
#      - AfricaFertilizer.org country dashboard CSV exports if exposed
#        (the seriesByProducts endpoint we already pull is retail PRICE only
#        not consumption volume).
#   2. Extract rows of the form state × year × nutrient × tonnes.
#   3. Map to schema below; emit one row per (state
#   4. Commit with the source PDF URL in the commit message.
# SCHEMA (canonical fertilizer_use):
#   source            = 'manual_import_nga_consumption'
#   source_record_id  = composite
#   country_iso3      = 'NGA'
#   country_name      = 'Nigeria'
#   state_or_region   = state name (e.g. 'Kano'
#   year              = calendar year of effect (use start year of agricultural-year ranges)
#   nutrient          = 'N' | 'P2O5' | 'K2O' | 'total'
#   total_tonnes      = consumption in metric tonnes; NULL if only kg/ha reported
#   kg_per_ha_arable  = consumption in kg/ha arable; NULL if only tonnes reported
#   arable_land_ha    = denominator used if kg_per_ha_arable populated
#   source_url        = direct URL to the source PDF/XLSX
#   retrieved_at      = ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of extraction
#   review_flags      = always include 'manual_import'; add caveats like 'NBS_1994_2015_series' or 'IFDC_FSO_2018_22'
# CURRENT STATE: header-only. Awaiting first analyst extraction.
